edges.description: Section A discusses the regulatory requirement for technical specifications on primary and secondary coolant iodine activity, which must be addressed by the design and operation of the reactor coolant system and its components as described in Section B, including the steam generators that are integral to the Rankine cycle.

Section A discusses regulatory conformance regarding technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity, while Section B provides test data and validation for the thermal-hydraulic performance of the steam generator, which supports demonstrating that the secondary conditions (including iodine activity) can be controlled as required.

Section A discusses the technical specification requirements for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity in the context of radiological consequences, while Section B addresses the maintenance of water quality in the secondary system to prevent corrosion and radioactivity releases; both sections are connected through their focus on ensuring the integrity and safety of the secondary side conditions relevant to the Rankine cycle and steam generator operation.

Section A discusses technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant iodine activity relevant to radiological consequences, while Section B addresses maintaining secondary water quality to prevent corrosion and associated failures, both of which are connected through their focus on ensuring the integrity and safety of the secondary side of the steam generator system in the NuScale Rankine cycle.

Section B provides critical heat flux performance data for the steam generator, which supports the design basis and operational parameters of the steam generators described in Section A, thereby ensuring the Rankine cycle's secondary conditions are validated.

Section A describes the design and components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators that set the secondary side conditions, while Section B lists the regulatory requirements and conformance statements for secondary system components, thus connecting the design basis (A) to the regulatory criteria (B) that must be satisfied.

Section A describes the design and components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B lists the regulatory requirements and conformance statements for secondary-side systems (such as water quality and material requirements) that the design must satisfy.

Section A provides test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel's thermal-hydraulic performance, which supports demonstrating that the Rankine cycle design (Ch.01) can satisfy the secondary system water quality and material requirements outlined in Section B's referenced regulatory criteria (Ch.05).

Section A provides test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design's thermal-hydraulic performance, which supports Section B's demonstration of conformance with regulatory criteria for secondary system integrity and water quality in the Rankine cycle.

Section A and Section B both reference SRP 10.4.6 but distinguish between direct cycle BWR (A) and indirect cycle PWR (B) water quality requirements for corrosion protection, highlighting the regulatory criteria relevant to the NuScale Rankine cycle's secondary conditions.

Section A discusses the performance and conformance of the NuScale Rankine cycle and related secondary-side events, while Section B addresses the technical specifications for secondary coolant system iodine activity, both of which are connected through the secondary system operating conditions that must be maintained to ensure regulatory compliance and safety during abnormal events.

Section A discusses the performance and regulatory conformance of the NuScale Rankine cycle under various transient conditions, while Section B details the design and configuration of the reactor coolant system and steam generators, thus Section B provides the technical basis and boundary conditions that Section A's analysis must satisfy.

Section A discusses the performance and regulatory conformance of the NuScale Rankine cycle under various transient conditions, while Section B provides supporting thermal-hydraulic test data for the steam generator's secondary-side performance, which is necessary to validate that the Rankine cycle is appropriately matched to the steam generator's secondary conditions.

Both Section A and Section B discuss NuScale's conformance with specific SRP regulatory criteria, with Section A addressing containment system requirements and Section B addressing radiological consequences and technical specifications for coolant system iodine activity, thus both are connected by their evaluation of NuScale's compliance with NRC safety requirements for different but related plant systems.

Section A discusses the partial conformance of the NuScale containment system with regulatory criteria for fission product control, while Section B describes the design and components of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, which are relevant to the secondary conditions referenced in the analysis question.

Section B provides technical testing data (CHF data and correlations) for the NuScale fuel design, which supports the evaluation of whether the Rankine cycle in Section A is appropriately matched to the steam generator secondary conditions by supplying evidence of thermal-hydraulic performance.

Section A documents the NuScale FSAR's overall conformance with SRP and DSRS requirements, while Section B provides a specific evaluation of conformance with SRP 15.6.3 regarding secondary coolant system iodine activity, thus Section A references and summarizes the more detailed compliance information found in Section B.

Section A documents the NuScale design's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides the technical design details of the reactor coolant system and steam generators, thus Section B's information supports the claims of regulatory conformance made in Section A regarding system design.

Section A documents the NuScale design's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B provides technical test data (CHF testing) that supports the validation of secondary system performance claims referenced in Section A.

Section A provides analytical parameters and conformance statements for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures under various transient conditions, which are directly relevant to Section B's focus on plant-specific technical specifications for primary and secondary coolant system iodine activity, as these pressures and conditions affect the potential for steam generator tube failure and subsequent radiological consequences.

Section A references the analytical parameters and regulatory conformance for system pressures and heat transfer in accident scenarios, while Section B specifies the detailed design and configuration of the reactor coolant system and steam generators, thus Section B provides the technical basis for the parameters evaluated in Section A.

Section B provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which supports the analytical parameters and safety analysis results in Section A by ensuring that the steam generator secondary conditions and reactor coolant system performance are based on validated thermal-hydraulic data.

Section A provides analytical model parameter values and conformance for transient events in the NuScale Rankine cycle, while Section B specifies technical specification requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity, which must be considered in the analytical modeling of secondary system conditions described in Section A.

Section A discusses the analytical model parameters and regulatory conformance for transient events in the NuScale system, while Section B describes the physical design and configuration of the reactor coolant system and steam generators, thus Section B provides the technical design context that Section A's analytical models and regulatory evaluations are based on.

Section A outlines the analytical model parameters and regulatory conformance for transient events in the NuScale Rankine cycle, while Section B provides critical heat flux testing data for the steam generator's secondary side, which serves as technical evidence supporting the validity of the analytical models and their parameter values in Section A.

Section A discusses the performance and regulatory conformance of the NuScale Rankine cycle and related secondary-side events, while Section B addresses containment and fission product control systems, with both sections referencing the NuScale FSAR's alignment with regulatory criteria but focusing on different safety functions (secondary system performance vs. containment integrity).

Section A discusses NuScale's conformance with DSRS 15.2.1-15.2.5 requirements for system performance under specific transients, while Section B provides a tabular summary indicating conformance with the same DSRS and SRP sections, thus Section B supports and summarizes the detailed compliance claims made in Section A.

Section A discusses the performance and conformance of nonsafety-related systems (including the Rankine cycle) under various transient conditions, while Section B specifies the analytical parameters and pressure conditions for the reactor coolant and main steam systems during those same transients, thus Section A's evaluation of system performance is based on the conditions and parameters detailed in Section B.

Section A discusses the performance and conformance of nonsafety-related systems under specific transient events, while Section B provides the values of parameters used in the analytical model for those same events, thus Section B specifies the technical details that underpin the assessments made in Section A.

Section A discusses NuScale's partial conformance with SRP 6.5.3 due to design differences, while Section B provides a summary table indicating the conformance status of various SRP sections, including references to the same criteria, thus Section B references and summarizes the detailed conformance information found in Section A.

Section A discusses the containment systems and their conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B details the analytical parameters and pressure conditions for the reactor coolant and main steam systems, both referencing NuScale's Final Safety Analysis Report and regulatory conformance, thus Section A provides structural context for containment that supports the operational scenarios analyzed in Section B.

Section A documents the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory criteria, while Section B details specific DSRS analytical parameters and their conformance status, thus Section A provides a general summary that references and encompasses the more detailed requirements and results found in Section B.

Section A documents that the NuScale FSAR conforms to the DSRS sections referenced in Section B, indicating that the analytical models and parameter values (including those for the Rankine cycle and SG secondary conditions) are aligned with regulatory requirements.

Section A lists analytical parameters (including core burnup) and their conformance for various transient scenarios, while Section B provides the specific values of these parameters (such as core burnup and initial power level) used in the analytical model for the same scenarios, thus Section B specifies the detailed parameter values referenced in Section A's summary of conformance.

Section A describes the design and components of the NuScale steam generators, which are directly relevant to Section B's discussion of regulatory requirements for secondary coolant system iodine activity, as the SG secondary conditions must align with these technical specifications to ensure radiological safety.

Section A and Section B are identical excerpts describing the components within the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, and thus provide the same design information relevant to evaluating the compatibility of the Rankine cycle with SG secondary conditions.

Section B provides critical heat flux (CHF) test data and correlations for the NuScale fuel design, which supports the thermal-hydraulic performance claims of the steam generators described in Section A, ensuring that the Rankine cycle's secondary conditions are based on validated fuel and SG performance.

Section A discusses compliance with ASME and NRC codes for overpressure protection and inservice testing, while Section B addresses conformance with regulatory criteria for radiological consequences and coolant system iodine activity, connecting them through the shared reliance on regulatory compliance and technical specifications for safe operation.

Section A outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) governing the design and testing of reactor coolant boundary components, which Section B applies in describing the specific design and configuration of the reactor coolant system and its subsystems, including the steam generators relevant to the Rankine cycle.

Section A outlines compliance with ASME and NRC regulatory codes for reactor coolant boundary integrity, while Section B details thermal-hydraulic performance and critical heat flux testing for the steam generator, both of which are prerequisites for ensuring that the Rankine cycle design in Ch.01 is compatible with the SG secondary conditions in Ch.05.
